metadata.summary: Reference combining three validation lenses: Problem Validation (evidence hierarchy + 5-step process + PROCEED/PIVOT/KILL decision), Pain Point Mining (tiered source strategy + Pain Intensity Matrix), and Niche Viability Scoring (5-criteria weighted model). All three are pre-build tools; none apply after product-market fit.

content.title: Validation Methods
content.summary: A reference combining three validation lenses: Problem Validation (evidence hierarchy + 5-step process + PROCEED/PIVOT/KILL decision), Pain Point Mining (tiered source strategy + Pain Intensity Matrix scoring), and Niche Viability Scoring (5-criteria weighted model: market size 25%, competition 20%, barriers 20%, profitability 20%, your fit 15%). All three are pre-build tools; none apply after product-market fit.

why.p: Early-stage discovery requires multiple simultaneous lenses: a problem may be real (validation) and painful (pain mining) but in a market too small or crowded to sustain a business (niche viability). Running only one lens produces an incomplete picture. The 5-criteria niche model adds a dimension not present in the other methodologies: the researcher's own fit, which affects execution risk independently of market attractiveness.

list.item: Early discovery phase: before any code or design work begins
Deciding whether to pivot a product idea or kill it
Evaluating a new niche against a portfolio of opportunities
After user complaints surface — checking if a recurring pain is widespread
Pre-sprint: confirming a feature addresses a real problem before scoping
When you already have paying customers validating with their wallets — move to retention analysis
When iterating on an existing shipped feature — switch to analytics and usage data
When the hypothesis is too vague to score against the 5-criteria model
For internal tooling with a captive user base who must use the product

section.name: problem-validation
section.p: Evidence types by strength (from most reliable to least): paid/committed (customer paid for competitor or solution), engaged (actively seeking the solution), stated (customer says they have the problem), observed (researcher sees the problem in use). Scored on 5-step process: (1) define the problem hypothesis precisely, (2) identify evidence sources by type, (3) collect evidence from each source, (4) score each evidence piece by strength, (5) output PROCEED/PIVOT/KILL recommendation with confidence level.

rule.statement: Collect evidence from at least 3 independent source types before scoring; single-source validation is fragile.
rule.rationale: Different sources have different biases (Reddit skews toward pain; Twitter toward aspiration; interviews toward social desirability).

rule.statement: Use verbatim quotes as primary evidence — paraphrases lose signal in synthesis.
rule.rationale: Direct customer language reveals emotional intensity and specificity; paraphrases flatten both.

rule.statement: Apply the 5-criteria model AFTER evidence collection, not during, to avoid confirmation bias.
rule.rationale: Bias during evidence collection leads to selective attention and missed contradictions.

section.name: pain-mining-and-niche
section.p: Pain mining uses a tiered source strategy: Tier 1 (high confidence) = paid alternatives, Feature Requests (GitHub/ProductHunt), Support Tickets. Tier 2 (medium confidence) = Reddit/StackOverflow/forums, App Store reviews, G2 Crowd. Tier 3 (low confidence) = Twitter/LinkedIn, SurveyMonkey, general web search. Pain Intensity Matrix scores each pain on frequency (daily/weekly/monthly/annual) × severity (extreme/moderate/minor) to identify the top 3-5 pains to validate. Niche Viability Scoring is a 5-criteria weighted model: market size (25%) — TAM from Statista, SimilarWeb, Gartner; competition (20%) — number and maturity of direct competitors; barriers to entry (20%) — capital/expertise/network required; profitability (20%) — unit economics and pricing power; your fit (15%) — execution capability, domain expertise, unfair advantage. Each criterion scored 1-5; multiply by weight; threshold for proceeding is weighted score ≥3.5.

rule.statement: For niche scoring, research market size from multiple proxies (search volume + TAM reports + competitor revenue estimates) and take the geometric mean.
rule.rationale: Single proxy is prone to extremes; geometric mean dampens outliers and is more robust than arithmetic mean.

rule.statement: Record your Proceed/Pivot/Kill decision log with the evidence snapshot so future agents can re-evaluate as new data arrives.
rule.rationale: Decision log enables learning and prevents "sunk cost fallacy" as new evidence emerges.

rule.statement: Pain-point mining is most reliable on platforms where users are not trying to sell something (Reddit > LinkedIn).
rule.rationale: Sellers bias their language; genuine users express pain more directly.

antipattern.description: Agents will hallucinate search volume numbers if the search API is not provided; always inject real data as context.
antipattern.reason: LLMs are unreliable on numeric data; grounding with real APIs or datasets prevents fabrication.

antipattern.description: G2 and Trustpilot block automated scraping aggressively — use cached Apify datasets or rate-limit to 1 req/5s.
antipattern.reason: IP bans and legal risk; cached datasets are more sustainable.

antipattern.description: Pain-point categorization by LLM is inconsistent across runs; use a fixed taxonomy and ask the agent to classify, not generate categories.
antipattern.reason: Without a fixed taxonomy, the same pain gets labeled differently across runs, breaking aggregation.

antipattern.description: Niche viability scoring requires the agent to have explicit market size data; without it, the agent will anchor on vague impressions and produce unreliable scores.
antipattern.reason: LLMs amplify uncertain inputs into false confidence; explicit data grounds the analysis.

template.purpose: Problem Validation Report with evidence table and decision
LLM prompt to score evidence and output a filled report
LLM prompt to return raw niche scores as JSON
PRAW-based Reddit pain aggregator by keyword theme
